vulnerabilities.title: Command Injection via execSync in github-safe.js
vulnerabilities.description: The script uses execSync to execute shell commands with user-supplied arguments without proper escaping. Vulnerable pattern: `execSync(`gh ${command} ${subcommand} ${newArgs.join(' ')}`)
vulnerabilities.exploitVector: Attack via crafted command arguments containing shell metacharacters (;, &&, ||, backticks, $())
vulnerabilities.impact: Remote Code Execution (RCE) with process privileges
vulnerabilities.remediation: Replace string interpolation with array-based spawn or use execFileSync instead of execSync

vulnerabilities.title: Hardcoded Test Credentials in Source Code
vulnerabilities.description: Multiple hardcoded test credentials including couple secretKey='test-secret-123' and passwords='password123' visible in version control and seed scripts
vulnerabilities.affectedCode: const couple = await prisma.couple.create({ data: { secretKey: 'test-secret-123', ... } })
vulnerabilities.exploitVector: Credentials visible in GitHub, CI logs, or if seed script is accidentally run against production
vulnerabilities.impact: Account takeover, unauthorized data access, authentication bypass
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use environment variables (process.env.TEST_SECRET) with random generation per run

vulnerabilities.title: Known CVEs in Production Dependencies
vulnerabilities.description: Multiple critical vulnerabilities in esbuild, minimatch, and vite: ReDoS attacks, RCE via registry manipulation, and arbitrary request execution
vulnerabilities.exploitVector: Dependency confusion attacks, crafted input triggering ReDoS, compromised NPM registry packages
vulnerabilities.impact: Application crash, RCE, supply chain compromise
vulnerabilities.remediation: Run 'npm audit fix --force' for frontend (upgrade vite to 8.0.16+), 'npm audit fix' for backend

vulnerabilities.title: Hardcoded Demo Credentials in Archive Script
vulnerabilities.description: Demo script contains full account credentials in plaintext: emails (alice@test.com, bob@test.com), passwords, and API endpoints
vulnerabilities.exploitVector: Script leaked or discovered in public repositories, CI logs, or backups
vulnerabilities.impact: Account takeover, unauthorized API access, test data manipulation
vulnerabilities.remediation: Delete archive/DEMO_SCRIPT.js or convert to example with placeholder values ([[REDACTED]])

vulnerabilities.title: Insufficient CORS Origin Validation
vulnerabilities.description: CORS middleware allows requests without Origin header (`!origin || allowedOrigins.includes(origin)`) combined with credentials=true, enabling credentialed CSRF from any source
vulnerabilities.affectedCode: cors({ origin: (o, cb) => !origin || allowedOrigins.includes(o) ? cb(null, true) : cb(null, false), credentials: true })
vulnerabilities.exploitVector: POST requests from untrusted origins without Origin header (mobile apps, form submissions) with credentials=true
vulnerabilities.impact: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) via credentialed requests
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the !origin fallback - require explicit Origin header: `if (allowedOrigins.includes(origin)) return cb(null, true); return cb(new Error('CORS not allowed'))`

vulnerabilities.title: Debug Information Leakage in Production
vulnerabilities.description: Account deletion code exposed in JSON response instead of console-only when NODE_ENV !== 'production'
vulnerabilities.affectedCode: return res.json({ ok: true, codeViaConsole: true, code })
vulnerabilities.exploitVector: Staging environments or misconfigured NODE_ENV variables exposing sensitive deletion codes in API responses
vulnerabilities.impact: Information disclosure, account takeover if codes are reused
vulnerabilities.remediation: Only log to console in dev: if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development') console.log('code:', code); return res.json({ ok: true })
cwe: CWE-532: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File

vulnerabilities.title: Test Credentials Hardcoded in E2E Helpers
vulnerabilities.description: Test password hardcoded as 'pwd12345' in E2E test helper function, exposed in CI/CD logs and test reports
vulnerabilities.exploitVector: CI/CD log exposure, test report leakage, git history mining
vulnerabilities.impact: Test account takeover if test credentials are reused in staging
vulnerabilities.remediation: Generate random test password per run or inject via environment variable: process.env.E2E_TEST_PASSWORD || generateRandomPassword()

vulnerabilities.title: Insufficient Rate Limiting on Sensitive Operations
vulnerabilities.description: Sensitive endpoints (account deletion, invitation creation, event bulk operations) lack strict rate limiting
vulnerabilities.affectedCode: Account deletion, invitation sending, premium interest signup endpoints
vulnerabilities.exploitVector: Brute force on deletion codes, spam invitation emails, bulk account enumeration
vulnerabilities.impact: Denial of Service, spam, harassment, account takeover
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement strict rate limiting: deletion (1 per day), invitations (5 per day), events (50 per hour)
cwe: CWE-770: Allocation of Resources Without Limits

vulnerabilities.title: Use of Unsafe Raw Query in Migration Check
vulnerabilities.description: Uses $queryRawUnsafe for hardcoded query string - while safe here (no user input), method indicates unsafe pattern that could be copy-pasted elsewhere
vulnerabilities.affectedCode: const row = await prisma.$queryRawUnsafe<...>(`SELECT migration_name FROM "_prisma_migrations" ...`)
vulnerabilities.exploitVector: If query is modified to include user input without proper escaping
vulnerabilities.impact: Potential SQL injection if pattern is reused with user input
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use typed query or $queryRaw with parameterized queries: prisma.$queryRaw`SELECT ... WHERE id = ${userId}`

vulnerabilities.title: Unvalidated Data Attribute in Event Handler
vulnerabilities.description: DOM data attributes read without validation before storing in global state: `window.selectedChoice = el.dataset.choice`
vulnerabilities.exploitVector: If data-choice is dynamically generated from user input without sanitization, could enable XSS
vulnerabilities.impact: Cross-Site Scripting if data attributes are user-controllable
vulnerabilities.remediation: Validate choice values against whitelist: const validChoices = ['a', 'b', 'c']; if (!validChoices.includes(el.dataset.choice)) throw Error(...)

vulnerabilities.title: Weak Temporary File Naming on Windows
vulnerabilities.description: Temporary files created in shared tmpdir with randomBytes(8) - Windows temp directory is world-readable with predictable timing window
vulnerabilities.exploitVector: Race condition to read temp file before deletion, predictable tmp paths on Windows
vulnerabilities.impact: Information disclosure of GitHub body content (could contain sensitive data)
vulnerabilities.remediation: Increase randomness (16 bytes), set strict file permissions (0600), use OS secure temp API
cwe: CWE-330: Use of Insufficiently Random Values

vulnerabilities.title: Insufficient Password Strength in Tests
vulnerabilities.description: Test uses only 8-character password ('pwd12345'), suggests backend may accept weak passwords
vulnerabilities.exploitVector: If backend accepts passwords <12 characters, weak password dictionary attacks succeed
vulnerabilities.impact: Brute force password cracking, user account compromise

vulnerabilities.title: Test Credentials in CI/CD Logs
vulnerabilities.description: Test output includes screenshots and trace files that may contain authentication tokens, session IDs, or other sensitive data
vulnerabilities.exploitVector: CI/CD log artifacts exposed, test reports accessible to unauthorized users, GitHub Actions artifact exposure
vulnerabilities.impact: Session hijacking via leaked tokens, account impersonation, test data leakage
vulnerabilities.remediation: Redact sensitive data from screenshots, mask tokens in traces, use secure artifact storage with RBAC
cwe: CWE-532: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File

vulnerabilities.title: Incomplete HSTS Configuration
vulnerabilities.description: HSTS header (Strict-Transport-Security) only configured conditionally, may not be enforced in all environments
vulnerabilities.affectedCode: helmet() applied selectively based on environment
vulnerabilities.exploitVector: Man-in-the-middle attacks on staging/development servers, mixed content loading
vulnerabilities.impact: Browser fails to enforce HTTPS-only connection on repeat visits
vulnerabilities.remediation: Enable HSTS in all non-local environments: 'Strict-Transport-Security': 'max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload'

vulnerabilities.title: Incomplete Refresh Token Rotation Integration
vulnerabilities.description: While refresh token rotation is implemented (rotatedFrom field exists), frontend integration may not consistently rotate tokens on every request
vulnerabilities.affectedCode: refreshTokenService.rotate() logic exists but may not be called on every refresh
vulnerabilities.exploitVector: Long-lived tokens if refresh endpoint not called regularly, token reuse attacks
vulnerabilities.impact: Extended token lifetime increases exposure window if token is leaked
vulnerabilities.remediation: Verify frontend calls refresh endpoint on every request or at least every 5 minutes; implement token expiration monitoring

actions.details: Replace execSync with execFileSync using array arguments. No shell interpolation.
actions.codeChange: Replace: execSync(`gh ${args.join(' ')}`) with: execFileSync('gh', args, { stdio: 'inherit' })

actions.details: Delete all hardcoded secrets from seed.js and DEMO_SCRIPT.js. Use crypto.randomBytes() for generation.
Run full npm audit and apply critical fixes. Upgrade esbuild, minimatch, vite to patched versions.
Remove !origin fallback that allows requests without Origin header
actions.codeChange: if (allowedOrigins.includes(origin)) return cb(null, true); return cb(new Error('CORS not allowed'))

actions.details: Create fixture that generates random test credentials per run, never hardcoded
Implement per-user rate limits on: account deletion (1/day), invitations (5/day), events (100/hour)
Delete archive/DEMO_SCRIPT.js or convert to README with placeholder values
actions.command: git rm archive/DEMO_SCRIPT.js && git commit -m 'security: remove hardcoded credentials from demo script'

actions.details: Add git-secrets and TruffleHog to pre-commit hooks and CI/CD pipeline
setup: Add to GitHub Actions: uses: trufflesecurity/trufflehog@main

actions.details: Redact sensitive data from screenshots/traces in CI. Implement token masking in logs.
actions.implementation: Create CI script to redact JWT tokens from HTML reports before upload

actions.details: Find all execSync/exec calls. Replace with spawn/execFileSync where possible.
Enforce 12+ character passwords with uppercase, lowercase, number, symbol requirements
Configure HSTS with preload directive for all environments except localhost
Audit frontend to ensure refresh token called on every request or 5-min interval
actions.verification: Check API logs for refresh endpoint call frequency

actions.details: Schedule recurring dependency checks. Update vulnerable packages immediately.
actions.automation: GitHub Dependabot or renovate bot configuration

positiveFindings.finding: bcrypt configured with ROUNDS=12 (strong cost factor)
positiveFindings.impact: Password hashing is computationally expensive, resistant to brute force

positiveFindings.finding: Helmet.js configured for XSS, CSP, clickjacking protections
Prisma ORM used (prevents SQL injection when not using unsafe queries)
Soft-delete with deletedAt field prevents data loss
positiveFindings.impact: Can recover deleted data for compliance audits

positiveFindings.finding: No dangerouslySetInnerHTML, eval(), or Function() constructors found
positiveFindings.impact: Low XSS attack surface in React components

positiveFindings.finding: Refresh token rotation mechanism with rotatedFrom tracking
positiveFindings.impact: Can detect and invalidate compromised token chains

nextSteps.immediate: Schedule security incident response meeting, assign owners to CRITICAL items
nextSteps.week1: Complete CRITICAL and HIGH remediations, verify with security re-scan
nextSteps.week2: Deploy fixes, test in staging, prepare security advisory if public disclosure needed
nextSteps.ongoing: Implement automated scanning, monthly audits, security training for team
